mapping.a_element: deterministic Levin-Segel reaction-diffusion dynamics that fluctuations are measured around
mapping.b_element: McKean-Vlasov mean-field PDE that the particle system converges to

mapping.a_element: demographic-noise fluctuations of the multispecies density field
mapping.b_element: fluctuations of the empirical measure of the N-particle system

mapping.a_element: number variance in a window of size R as the fluctuation observable
mapping.b_element: central-limit-theorem variance / Berry-Esseen bound for empirical interaction fields

brief_justification: Both analyze Gaussian fluctuations of a stochastic many-body system around a deterministic mean dynamics, but the transferable machinery differs: paper A's core mechanism is hyperuniformity, an anomalous suppression of long-wavelength density fluctuations (1/R number-variance scaling) driven by demographic noise near a Turing instability, whereas paper B's is a rigorous propagation-of-chaos / relative-entropy (Krylov-Khasminskii) plus CLT method for the N->infinity mean-field limit of singular-kernel McKean-Vlasov SDEs. Neither the Turing-instability/hyperuniformity structure of A nor the singular-kernel mean-field-limit method of B maps onto the other, so the overlap is only the generic 'fluctuations of a stochastic system yield a CLT'.
